frameworks.name: structure learning
frameworks.one_line: learning relations among objects and categories in a scene

frameworks.name: relational inference / low-dimensional manifold coding
frameworks.one_line: PPC embeds high-dim inputs on a manifold encoding relative position and abstract relations

frameworks.name: deep learning models of vision
frameworks.one_line: current deep nets lack relational inference and global scene understanding

frameworks.name: embodied / action-grounded cognition
frameworks.one_line: structure learning grounded in reach and eye-fixation actions

core_question: How does the primate parietal cortex support structure learning by representing relations among objects and abstract entities?
key_assumptions: primate dorsal stream performs relational inference
PPC encodes inputs on a low-dimensional manifold
relations generalise across spatial and nonspatial domains
key_findings: review evidence that PPC represents relative positions of objects in physical space
PPC also encodes relations among entities in abstract conceptual space
low-dimensional code supports generalisation of relational information beyond spatial domains
interpretations: parietal cortex is critical for relational structure learning underpinning scene understanding
structure learning is grounded in object-directed actions (reaching, fixation)
